name: Haili's Hawaiian Food
tagline: Family-recipe Hawaiian food since 1950 — laulau, kalua pig, and squid luau that rival Helena's without the hype.
mustOrder: Laulau — tender pork wrapped in taro and ti leaves, steamed for hours
Kalua pig plate — smoky, salty, fall-apart pulled pork
Squid luau — squid simmered in creamy taro leaf
parking: Small lot in front on North King St. Street parking available
bestTime: Lunch rush (11am–1pm) can have lines. Go early or after 2pm
talkStory: Founded in 1950 by the Haili family on North King Street in Palama, this is one of the oldest continuously operating Hawaiian food restaurants on Oahu. The recipes — laulau, kalua pig, squid luau, haupia — are unchanged family originals passed down through generations. Locals consider it the 'no-line alternative' to Helena's, with food that's just as authentic. The neighborhood is deep local Honolulu that tourists never see, which is exactly the point.
